title: rosa communis var. platyphylla
intro: Rosa communis var. platyphylla, also known as the broad-leaved dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa communis, a species of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of salicylic acid, which is a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound. The broad-leaved dog rose has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
